chapter: Chapter: Electricity – Magnetic and Heating Effects

questions.question: Explain why an electric circuit must be closed for current to flow.
questions.answer: An electric circuit must be closed to provide a continuous path for electric charges to move from the source through the components and back to the source.

questions.question: What is the role of a battery in an electric circuit?
questions.answer: A battery supplies electrical energy and pushes electric charges through the circuit.

questions.question: Why are copper wires commonly used in electrical circuits?
questions.answer: Copper wires are used because copper is a good conductor of electricity and allows current to flow easily.

questions.question: What happens when too much current flows through a wire?
questions.answer: The wire becomes very hot due to the heating effect of electric current and may melt or cause damage.

questions.question: Explain the heating effect of electric current with an example.
questions.answer: The heating effect of electric current is the production of heat when current flows through a conductor. For example, an electric iron becomes hot when current passes through it.

questions.question: Why is a fuse important in an electric circuit?
questions.answer: A fuse protects electrical appliances by melting and breaking the circuit when excessive current flows.

questions.question: How does an electromagnet differ from a permanent magnet?
questions.answer: An electromagnet produces magnetism only when electric current flows through it, while a permanent magnet has magnetism all the time.

questions.question: What is the magnetic effect of electric current?
questions.answer: The magnetic effect of electric current is the phenomenon where a current-carrying conductor produces a magnetic field around it.

questions.question: What observation shows that a current-carrying wire produces a magnetic field?
questions.answer: The needle of a compass placed near a current-carrying wire gets deflected.

questions.question: Why is soft iron used as the core of an electromagnet?
questions.answer: Soft iron is used because it becomes strongly magnetized when current flows and loses magnetism quickly when current stops.

questions.question: Name two devices that work using the heating effect of electric current.
questions.answer: Electric iron and electric heater work using the heating effect of electric current.

questions.question: Why are electric wires coated with insulating material?
questions.answer: Insulating materials prevent electric shocks and stop current from escaping the wire.

questions.question: Explain how an electric bell works.
questions.answer: An electric bell works using an electromagnet that attracts an iron armature when current flows, producing a ringing sound.

questions.question: Why does an electric bulb glow when current flows through it?
questions.answer: The filament inside the bulb gets heated to a very high temperature due to the heating effect of current and produces light.

questions.question: What is meant by a current-carrying conductor?
questions.answer: A current-carrying conductor is a wire or material through which electric current is flowing.

questions.question: Why is the heating effect useful in electrical appliances?
questions.answer: The heating effect is useful for appliances like heaters, irons and toasters that require heat for their operation.

questions.question: What happens to an electromagnet when electric current stops flowing?
questions.answer: The electromagnet loses its magnetism when the current stops.

questions.question: Why is electricity considered an important form of energy?
questions.answer: Electricity is important because it can easily be converted into light, heat, and mechanical energy.

questions.question: How can an electromagnet be made stronger?
questions.answer: An electromagnet can be made stronger by increasing the number of turns of the coil or increasing the current.

questions.question: Why is electric safety important at home?
questions.answer: Electric safety is important to prevent electric shocks, fires, and damage to appliances.

questions.question: What would happen if the insulating layer of a wire is damaged?
questions.answer: It may cause electric shocks or short circuits.

questions.question: Why are switches used in electrical circuits?
questions.answer: Switches are used to control the flow of electric current by opening or closing the circuit.

questions.question: Explain why metals are generally good conductors of electricity.
questions.answer: Metals allow electric charges to move freely through them, which helps electric current flow easily.

questions.question: Why does a fuse wire melt when excessive current flows?
questions.answer: A fuse wire melts because excessive current produces a large amount of heat due to the heating effect.

questions.question: Name two devices that use electromagnets.
questions.answer: Electric bell and electric crane use electromagnets.
